description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:stable image with the docker:dind service, define variables CONTAINER_IMAGE and DOCKER_DRIVER, and three stages: upgrade, build, and deploy. In the upgrade stage add an upgrade job that runs only on triggers, with a before_script installing apk packages git python3 python3-dev py3-pip npm, upgrading pip and installing boto3, installing json2md via npm, then cloning the repository, running python3 scripts/populate-json.py $AWS_ACCESS_KEY $AWS_SECRET_KEY, converting JSON to markdown with node scripts/json2md.js, committing the generated docs/content/initial-images.md and pushing it back to the repo. In the build stage add a build job that runs on pushes and tags, logs into the GitLab registry, registers QEMU multiarch, pulls any existing image, builds a new Docker image with build args AWS_ACCESS_KEY and AWS_SECRET_KEY, tags it as $CONTAINER_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG-$CI_COMMIT_SHA and $CONTAINER_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG, optionally tags with $CI_COMMIT_TAG, and pushes all tags. In the deploy stage add a deploy-prod job that uses the alpine:3.7 image, runs only on the master branch and tags, installs curl, downloads kubectl, writes the base64‑decoded $KUBE_CONFIG_PROD to $HOME/.kube/config, and updates the Kubernetes deployment pv-prod-pantahub-docs in the default namespace to use the image $CONTAINER_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG-$CI_COMMIT_SHA. Ensure the pipeline respects the only rules and uses the defined variables and secrets for AWS credentials and Kubernetes configuration.
